seoTitle: Create a Landing Page in HubSpot: Full Guide
metaDescription: Build and publish a HubSpot landing page with the drag-and-drop editor, a lead capture form, SEO settings, and a final mobile preview check.
intro: A landing page in HubSpot pulls together a responsive template, a lead capture form, and SEO settings into one publishable asset, all through the drag-and-drop editor. This guide walks through picking a template, arranging your content blocks, and connecting a form so visitor data flows into your CRM. It closes with checking your page settings and previewing on mobile before you go live.

steps.heading: Open the Landing Pages hub
steps.body: Go to Marketing in HubSpot and navigate to the Landing Pages directory.

steps.heading: Start a new page
steps.body: Click Create Page, choose Landing Page, and give it a clear internal name.

steps.heading: Pick a template
steps.body: Select a responsive 2026 campaign template that fits the goal of your page.

steps.heading: Arrange your content
steps.body: Use the drag-and-drop editor to place headers, text blocks, and visual assets.

steps.heading: Add a lead capture form
steps.body: Insert a form module to collect names, emails, and other details, then set post-submission redirect rules.

steps.heading: Configure SEO and publish
steps.body: Set your page title, URL slug, and meta description, check the mobile preview, then click Publish.

faq.question: Do I need to know code to build a HubSpot landing page?
faq.answer: No, the drag-and-drop editor lets you arrange headers, text, and forms without writing code.

faq.question: Can I customize the URL for my landing page?
faq.answer: Yes, the Settings tab lets you edit the page's URL slug along with the title and meta description.

faq.question: How does the lead capture form connect to my CRM?
faq.answer: Submissions from the form module automatically create or update contact records in your HubSpot CRM.

faq.question: Should I check my page on mobile before publishing?
faq.answer: Yes, running a responsive preview check helps catch layout issues and broken elements before the page goes live.
